MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-11-2017 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT SISIHANGALE AHOSIVI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF DERRICK DAVIS IS DENIED. ASSUMING WITHOUT DECIDING THAT MR. AHOSIVI SATISFIED HIS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BASED ON MR. DAVIS' FACTUALLY DEVOID DISCOVERY RESPONSES, THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. AHOSIVI ACTED WITH MALICE. ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY MR. DAVIS SHOWS THAT MR. AHOSIVI ATTACKED MR. DAVIS FROM BEHIND WITHOUT PROVOCATION AND THEN BEGAN STOMPING HIM. (DAVIS DEC. PARS. 6-7; ELLIOT DEC. PARS. 7-8.). THIS EVIDENCE IS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A FINDING BY CLEAR AND CONVINCING EVIDENCE THAT MR. AHOSIVI ACTED MALICIOUSLY. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (302)

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE AND PREMISES LIABILITY AND THE REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ALLEGED BY PLAINTIFF DERRICK DAVIS IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. BAE FAILED TO SATISFY ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES THAT ARE CITED IN BAE'S SEPARATE STATEMENT FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT MR. DAVID PROVIDED FACTUALLY DEVOID RESPONSES WERE NOT DIRECTED TOWARD THE NEGLIGENCE AND PREMISES LIABILITY CLAIMS AND REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES ASKED ABOUT THE ASSAULT AND BATTERY CLAIM AND FACTS SHOWING HOW BAE IS VICARIOUSLY LIABLE. THERE WERE NO QUESTIONS ABOUT THE NEGLIGENCE AND PREMISES LIABILITY CLAIMS, WHICH ARE BASED ON A DIRECT THEORY OF LIABILITY. (SCHEIDING V. DINWIDDIE CONST. CO. (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 64, 81 (SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN DID NOT SHIFT WHERE DEFENDANT FAILED TO ASK SPECIFIC INTERROGATORIES REGARDING THE PLAINTIFF'S WORK HISTORY AND ASBESTOS EXPOSURE).) THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: GORDON F. AIAVAO, CSR NO. 11216, EMAIL: GAIAVAO@SFTC.ORG. (302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Sisihangale Ahosivis Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Tiega Varlack of the law offices of Varlack Legal Services (510-397-2008) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Stephanie Rothberg of the law offices of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Sisihangale Ahosivi

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

DEFENDANT SISIHANGALE AHOSIVI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT and Motion For Summary Adjudication on the request for punitive damages alleged by plaintiff Derrick Davis is DENIED. Assuming without deciding that Mr. Ahosivi satisfied his initial burden of production based on Mr. Davis' factually devoid discovery responses, there is a triable issue whether Mr. Ahosivi acted with malice. Admissible evidence submitted by Mr. Davis shows that Mr. Ahosivi attacked Mr. Davis from behind without provocation and then began stomping him. (Davis Dec. pars. 6-7; Elliot Dec. pars. 7-8.). This evidence is sufficient to support a finding by clear and convincing evidence that Mr. Ahosivi acted maliciously. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Bae Systems San Francisco Ship Repair Incs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-11-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Tiega Varlack of the law offices of Varlack Legal Services (510-397-2008) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Stephanie Rothberg of the law offices of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Sisihangale Ahosivi

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter.

Court having reviewed the pleading and having heard oral argument, tentative ruling is confirmed as follows:

DEFENDANT BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Motion For Summary Adjudication on the causes of action for negligence and premises liability and the request for punitive damages alleged by plaintiff Derrick Davis is DENIED in its entirety. BAE failed to satisfy its initial burden of production. The special interrogatories that are cited in BAE's separate statement for the proposition that Mr. David provided factually devoid responses were not directed toward the negligence and premises liability claims and request for punitive damages. The special interrogatories asked about the assault and battery claim and facts showing how BAE is vicariously liable. There were no questions about the negligence and premises liability claims, which are based on a direct theory of liability. (Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64, 81 (summary judgment burden did not shift where defendant failed to ask specific interrogatories regarding the plaintiff's work history and asbestos exposure).) The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Gordon F. Aiavao, CSR No. 11216, email: gaiavao@sftc.org. (D302)

Defendants Notice Of And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TIEGA VARLACK OF THE VARLACK LEGAL SERVICES, TEL. NO. 1-510-397-2008 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEPHANIE J. ROTHBERG OF THE WALSWORTH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-544-7139 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS SISIHANGALE AHOSIVI AND BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR INC.'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint is DENIED. Punitive damages are adequately pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TIEGA VARLACK OF THE VARLACK LEGAL SERVICES, TEL. NO. 1-510-397-2008 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. STEPHANIE J. ROTHBERG OF THE WALSWORTH LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-544-7139 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS SISIHANGALE AHOSIVI AND BAE SYSTEMS SAN FRANCISCO SHIP REPAIR INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. Plaintiff's complaint is more than adequate. Negligence and premises liability: the complaint details how BAE should have known Ahosivi was dangerous. Intentional infliction of emotional distress: the complaint pleads that "BAE's supervisor watched as Ahosivi battered plaintiff and did nothing to stop the vicious attack"; this suffices to plead reckless disregard at a minimum. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru
